design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore seamless world, interact with NPCs to gather information, complete virtue-based quests, engage in real-time tactical combat to resolve conflicts
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral ambiguity between defending Britannia against gargoyle invaders and discovering the legitimacy of the gargoyle perspective; players must reconcile initial conflict with emergent peace quest
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Champion of virtue defending a fantasy realm, evolving into a peacemaker who bridges conflicting civilizations through understanding their shared moral systems
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Ultima VI explicitly rejects simplistic good vs. evil narratives by revealing gargoyles possess their own virtue system; the game's design philosophy centers on moral complexity and perspective-shifting as core gameplay outcomes, not optional narrative flavor

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Party-based composition allows tactical flexibility; real-time elements introduce execution demands; object manipulation and world interactivity create emergent solutions to encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Object manipulation and crafting system enables player-driven item creation and equipment modification; party composition and spell advancement provide character progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Shrine of Virtue locations gate progression; NPC dialogue trees unlock quests and information; object interaction reveals secrets and alternate paths
replayability_hooks: Multiple NPC perspectives reveal conflicting narratives about the gargoyle conflict
Virtue system creates moral choice paths affecting faction relationships
Seamless 1024x1024 tile world encourages exploration-based discovery over linear progression
Party composition variations enable different tactical approaches to encounters

world_building_techniques: NPC portrait-based dialogue system with unlimited conversation length (no token restrictions like earlier Ultimas)
Environmental storytelling through seamless world integration—towns, castles, dungeons all exist at same scale without loading screens
Virtue shrine locations and sacred texts establish philosophical framework both civilizations share
Object manipulation reveals lore through interaction (crafting, world state changes)
Dual perspective narrative: players discover gargoyle virtue system mirrors Britannia's, recontextualizing antagonists as protagonists of their own conflict

notable_bosses: Gargoyle sacrificial priests (opening encounter establishes threat and moral inversion—who are the real invaders?)

faction_indicators: Gargoyle faction vs. Britannia faction with explicitly parallel virtue systems
NPC alignment signals through dialogue reflecting which civilization they support
Shrine of Virtue allegiance indicates moral faction standing
danger_signals: Initial gargoyle invasion frames them as existential threat to player's civilization
Sacrifice altar opening positions player as victim-protagonist requiring vengeance
Captured shrines represent corrupted sacred spaces, escalating threat perception
environmental_storytelling: Sacred texts carried by gargoyle priests establish religious legitimacy parallel to Britannia's faith
Shrine locations and their occupation/liberation status visualize territorial conflict
Seamless world map eliminates abstraction—conflict zones appear as continuous geography, not separate instances
NPC spatial distribution shows how civilizations coexist or clash in shared spaces
